title: Replace per-request user lookup with request-scoped batched loader and Redis-backed cache
business_context: Every authenticated request does UserModel.findById(req.user.id) in auth middleware AND again in 3-5 downstream services that need user data. Single API call results in 5 user lookups. Need request-scoped deduplication plus a warm Redis cache.
constraints: Cache invalidation must be immediate on user update
Stale data must not be served
Cannot break middleware contract (req.user populated synchronously after middleware)
Must work with multiple Node instances

before_state.description: Repeated direct findById calls; no caching.

reasoning: Two layers: 1) request-scoped loader (Map<userId, Promise<User>>) attached to req - same request never queries twice. 2) Redis cache with explicit invalidation via Mongoose post-save hook. Cache key user:{id}. Hot read path: Redis GET, miss falls to Mongo, write-through to Redis. On user update, hook DELs the key (and emits invalidation event for other instances via pub/sub). Tradeoff vs read-through with TTL: stale window on profile changes is unacceptable for security-relevant fields like role.

after_state.description: Request-scoped loader + Redis cache with explicit invalidation.

files.purpose: Cache-aside repo with explicit invalidation
Attaches per-request memoized loaders to dedupe within a request
Auth middleware uses request-scoped loader so any downstream call to req.loaders.user is free
Mongoose hooks invalidate cache on writes
Subscribes to invalidation events - extension point for adding in-process caches later

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add userRepo with caching; middleware switched but services keep direct lookups
Step 2: Add request loader; auth uses it
Step 3: Migrate services to req.loaders.user
Step 4: Add lint rule against direct UserModel.findById outside dal/
Step 5: Monitor cache hit rate
testing_strategy: Cache hit test: hit endpoint twice with same user, assert Mongo called once. Invalidation test: update user, assert next read returns new data. Dedup test: request that triggers 5 services needing user - assert one DB or cache call. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-051 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Replace per-request user lookup with request-scoped batched loader and Redis-backed cache handles the demo path but misses the performance crisis boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and cache stale hit rate are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Add real-time draft autosave with conflict detection when multiple devices edit
business_context: Long-form post editor saves drafts every 30s. Users on two devices clobber each other. Need realtime autosave with last-edited-wins on same device, conflict prompt across devices.
constraints: Saves must not lose more than 5s of typing on connection loss
Conflict prompts must be clear, not pop unexpectedly
Latency from type to save must feel imperceptible

issues: Stale closure over body - first 30s save sends initial empty body

reasoning: Client tracks a local version (vector clock simplified to scalar). Sends save with expectedVersion. Server compares: if matches, accept and increment, broadcast new version+content to other devices in user's room. If mismatch, return 409 with current server state and client shows conflict UI. Debounce save to 2s after last keystroke. Tradeoff vs CRDT: total order is fine for single-author drafts; conflicts only happen across devices for the same user.

after_state.description: Versioned optimistic save with conflict response; socket broadcast across user devices; debounced.

files.purpose: Conditional update on version - 409 with current state on conflict; broadcasts to peer devices
Hook with versioned save, debounce, conflict state, manual resolve actions
Editor renders body, conflict prompt, and resolve buttons

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add version field to Draft with backfill default 0
Step 2: Switch controller to versioned update
Step 3: Roll out client with debounced save and conflict UI
Step 4: Add socket namespace
Step 5: Remove 30s interval save
testing_strategy: Conflict simulation: two clients with same starting version save different bodies - one succeeds, other gets 409 with current. Cross-device sync test: device A saves, device B socket receives draft:updated. Debounce test: rapid typing produces one save 2s after stopping. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. Assert both user-visible output and persistence/cache/event side effects. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-052 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Add real-time draft autosave with conflict detection when multiple devices edit handles the demo path but misses the realtime e2e boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and authorization denial count are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Migrate ad-hoc cron jobs from setInterval to declarative scheduler with leader election
business_context: Cron jobs (daily reports, hourly cleanup, weekly billing) run via setInterval. Multiple API instances mean each job runs N times. Need leader election so jobs run exactly once.
constraints: No new infrastructure (no separate scheduler service)
reasoning: Redis-based leader election via SET NX EX. Lease renewed every 5s; if leader dies, another takes over. Leader runs the scheduler which dispatches jobs based on cron expressions stored in code (declarative). Each job execution gets a row in JobRun with status. Tradeoff vs distributed lock per job (vs single leader for all): leader-per-job allows scheduler outage to kill all jobs; single leader is simpler. Acceptable.

after_state.description: Single elected leader runs cron-style scheduler; jobs declared with schedule + handler; per-run audit.

files.purpose: Lua-based atomic lease renewal; loses leadership if Redis says so
Cron-driven dispatcher with concurrency limit and JobRun audit
Wires leader election to scheduler lifecycle

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add leader+scheduler; existing setIntervals continue
Step 2: One job at a time, move out of setInterval into scheduler
Step 3: Verify exactly-one execution in multi-instance staging
Step 4: Remove all setIntervals
Step 5: Add operator UI showing JobRun history
testing_strategy: Single-fire test: 3 instances, assert only one runs daily-report. Failover test: kill leader, assert another instance takes leadership within 20s. Audit test: failure throws, assert JobRun marked failed with error message. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-053 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Migrate ad-hoc cron jobs from setInterval to declarative scheduler with leader election handles the demo path but misses the architecture migration boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and state drift incidents are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Replace nested useState arrays with normalized entity store backed by Map
business_context: Trello-style board holds lists, each containing cards. Current state is List[] with nested Card[]. Updating one card re-renders all lists. Need normalized state with entity tables.
constraints: Selector hooks must avoid unnecessary re-renders
Existing components shouldn't change API drastically

issues: Renaming one card causes every list to re-render

reasoning: Normalize: cardsById and listsById as Maps. Lists hold cardIds array. Updates by id are O(1). Selector hooks per entity ensure granular re-renders.

after_state.description: Normalized entity store with Map; selector hooks.

files.purpose: Normalized store with Map-backed entities; minimal allocation on updates
Granular selectors - components re-render only for the entity they read
Components subscribe per entity; renaming one card re-renders only that card

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add store + hooks alongside existing nested state
Step 2: Migrate Card component to useCard
Step 3: Migrate List to useList
Step 4: Replace top-level state with store
Step 5: Remove nested arrays from props
testing_strategy: Render-count test: rename one card, assert CardView for other cards did not re-render. Move test: assert source list and target list update but other lists don't. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-054 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Replace nested useState arrays with normalized entity store backed by Map handles the demo path but misses the state overhaul boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and render commit time are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Add export-to-CSV/Excel as async job with status polling and signed S3 URL
business_context: Users export reports up to 500k rows. Current sync export times out at 30s nginx limit. Need async job pattern: enqueue → process → notify → download via signed URL.
constraints: Must not lock up event loop

before_state.description: Sync export builds CSV in memory and streams as response.

reasoning: POST /exports enqueues an ExportJob, returns jobId. Worker streams Mongo cursor through fast-csv into a temp file, then uploads to S3, marks job done with download URL. Client polls GET /exports/:id or subscribes via socket. Signed URL expires in 1h.

after_state.description: Async export pattern with cursor streaming, S3 upload, signed download.

files.purpose: Enqueue export and return job id; status endpoint with ownership check
Cursor-streamed CSV upload to S3 via multipart; progress updates via processedRows
Hook starts export and polls every 2s until done
Button starts export and reflects progress + download link

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add ExportJob model and worker behind feature flag
Step 2: Add new endpoint alongside old
Step 3: Migrate UI to async export
Step 4: Keep old endpoint for small data sets if needed
Step 5: Sunset old endpoint
testing_strategy: Large export: enqueue 500k row export, assert completes without OOM. Progress test: poll during processing, assert processedRows increments. Auth test: try to fetch another user's job, assert 404. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-055 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Add export-to-CSV/Excel as async job with status polling and signed S3 URL handles the demo path but misses the feature addition with refactor boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and rollback elapsed minutes are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Build interactive Three.js timeline of git commits with branch lanes and hover details
business_context: DevTools team wants a visual git history for the engineering Slack bot - 5 years of repo history with branches. Need a 3D timeline with lane-per-branch, sphere-per-commit, hover details, drill-down on click.
constraints: Click on commit must open detail panel without disposing scene
Must run on mid-range laptop GPUs
reasoning: InstancedMesh of 30k spheres positioned by (timeIndex, branchIndex). Branch assignment by stable hash so visually consistent across loads. Raycast for hover/click - r3f surfaces instanceId. Hover and click do not mutate scene structure - only update React state for detail panel. Camera with OrbitControls; LOD via instance.visible if outside viewport.

after_state.description: Instanced spheres timeline, raycaster details, detail panel side-by-side.

files.purpose: Side-by-side: 3D timeline left, detail panel right; click updates panel without scene disposal

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add new route /git-timeline; old log remains
Step 2: Ingest commits via a backend job into DB
Step 3: Iterate on visual encoding with engineering
Step 4: Make default
testing_strategy: 30k FPS test: render 30k spheres, assert >55fps. Hover test: click known commit, assert detail panel updates. Author filter: filter to one author, assert visible count matches. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-056 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Build interactive Three.js timeline of git commits with branch lanes and hover details handles the demo path but misses the 3d integration boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and policy block rate are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Implement org-scoped invites with single-use signed tokens and email throttling
business_context: Org admins invite users via email. Current flow sends a plain URL with org id - guessable. Need signed single-use tokens, expiration, email throttle per recipient, audit, accept/decline flow.
constraints: Same email cannot be invited more than 3x per 24h
Accepting invite must create user if not exists
reasoning: Generate cryptographically random invite token, hash it for storage, link contains plain token in URL fragment. Recipient clicks link, frontend posts token to /accept, server hashes and looks up, checks status+expiration. Throttle counted in Redis per email. Audit on each send and accept.

after_state.description: Signed single-use token model, throttle, audit, accept flow.

files.purpose: Stores hashed invite tokens with expiration and status
Send hashed token, throttle per email, validate and consume on accept
Pulls token from URL fragment (never sent to server logs), posts to accept, strips from URL

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add Invite model and new endpoints alongside old plain-URL flow
Step 2: Send signed invites for new ones; old links still work briefly
Step 3: Invalidate any old plain-URL paths after 7 days
Step 4: Add admin view of pending/accepted invites
testing_strategy: Single-use test: accept token, try again, assert 409. Expiry test: backdate expiresAt, assert 410 on accept. Throttle test: invite same email 4x in 24h, assert 429 on 4th. URL fragment test: hash contents do not appear in network logs. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-057 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Implement org-scoped invites with single-use signed tokens and email throttling handles the demo path but misses the multi tenancy boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and policy block rate are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Fix unbounded socket room broadcast amplification choking a notification fan-out
business_context: After a marketing email, 50k users hit the app in 5 min. Each user joins notifications room. New posts trigger broadcasts; each broadcast at 50k recipients * 12 active socket nodes = 600k I/O ops. Server falls over. Need bounded fan-out.
constraints: Burst protection must not delay critical alerts
Must work with existing Redis adapter
reasoning: Shard the global room into N sub-rooms (e.g., by user id mod 32). Each user joins one shard. Broadcasts iterate shards with small delay between. For low-priority notifications, batch posts in 1s windows and emit as array. Critical alerts have a separate fast lane channel that does immediate fan-out. Tradeoff vs server-sent events: irrelevant - same fan-out problem.

after_state.description: Sharded rooms + batching for low priority + fast-lane channel for critical.

files.purpose: Sharded rooms + batching window + critical fast lane + setImmediate yield
Client handles both single (critical) and batched events

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add sharding; existing room stays as fallback
Step 2: Switch clients to shard rooms on connect
Step 3: Add batching window
Step 4: Verify load shedding via load test
Step 5: Remove legacy global room
testing_strategy: Load test: 50k clients connected, post created, assert backend CPU spikes < 30% (was 95%). Critical path test: post with critical:true, assert immediate emit not batched. Batch test: 100 posts in 1s, assert single post:batch emitted per shard. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-058 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Fix unbounded socket room broadcast amplification choking a notification fan-out handles the demo path but misses the performance crisis boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and rollback elapsed minutes are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Extract config from process.env into typed config module with validation at boot
business_context: process.env values read scattered through code, often as string with manual parsing. Typos cause silent failures (e.g., MAX_CONNECTIONS undefined coerces to 0). Need centralized typed config validated at startup.
constraints: Must fail boot with clear error if config invalid
Secrets must not appear in logs
Hot reload of config not required

issues: Silent fallback to 3000 if env missing
! assertion - crashes at runtime if missing

reasoning: Zod schema at boot validates env. Each consumer imports from config/index.ts which exports typed values. If anything missing/invalid, throws on startup with clear error listing problems. Secrets accessed only via getter that intentionally doesn't get toString'd; logger redacts known secret keys. Tradeoff vs separate config service: overkill - all values known at boot.

after_state.description: Single config module with Zod-validated schema, startup-time failure, secret redaction.

files.purpose: Schema with defaults, coercion, and secret-format checks
Validates at first import; throws with grouped error message
Uses validated typed config; redacted log on boot
Imports typed config - no env-var reads, no ! assertions

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add schema and config module; old process.env reads continue
Step 2: Convert one consumer at a time to import from config
Step 3: Add lint rule banning process.env outside config/index.ts
Step 4: Document required env vars in README
testing_strategy: Boot fail test: omit MONGO_URL, assert startup throws with clear message. Type test: TypeScript checks ensure consumers can't misuse config types. Redaction test: log boot config, assert secrets shown as [REDACTED]. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-059 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Extract config from process.env into typed config module with validation at boot handles the demo path but misses the architecture migration boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and query p95 latency are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Add request signing for webhooks emitted to customers with HMAC and replay protection
business_context: We send webhook events to customer endpoints (order.created, etc.). Currently signed with nothing. Customers cannot verify our origin. Need HMAC-SHA256 signatures, timestamp, and replay protection.
constraints: Signature method should follow Stripe/Slack patterns customers know
Customers must rotate signing secrets without downtime
Replays older than 5 min must be rejected
reasoning: Generate signature as HMAC-SHA256(timestamp + '.' + body) using webhook secret. Headers: x-signature, x-timestamp, x-event-id. Customer verifies by recomputing and checking constant-time equal. Idempotency via x-event-id - customers can dedupe. Rotation: each webhook has primary + secondary secret; signatures sent with both during rotation; remove secondary after customer confirms.

after_state.description: HMAC-signed webhooks with timestamp, rotation support, retry queue.

files.purpose: Sign with HMAC; support secondary secret for rotation; enqueue delivery
Code customers paste into their handlers - timestamp window + timing-safe compare
Worker handles retries via queue's built-in retry on throw

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add signature headers without enforcing; customers can verify but unsigned still works
Step 2: Communicate to customers; provide verification code samples
Step 3: Provide rotation UI - customers set new secret, system sends with both
Step 4: After grace period, audit which webhooks customers verified - encourage holdouts
Step 5: Migrate to retries via queue with exponential backoff
testing_strategy: Signature parity: generate signature in our code, verify with sample customer code, assert match. Replay test: send valid request 6 min later, customer verifier rejects on timestamp. Rotation test: secondary secret valid simultaneously. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-060 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Add request signing for webhooks emitted to customers with HMAC and replay protection handles the demo path but misses the security hardening boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and cache stale hit rate are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Replace global toast state with subscription-based toaster supporting groups and dismissal

constraints: ARIA live region for screen readers
reasoning: Module-scoped store with subscribe API. Toaster component subscribes and renders array. Dedup by key. Programmatic dismiss via returned id. ARIA live region for assistive tech.

after_state.description: Subscription-based toaster, dedup, dismiss, ARIA.

files.purpose: Subscription store with dedup by key, programmatic dismiss, auto-dismiss timers
Cross-env id generator
Renders all toasts with ARIA roles and dismiss button
Ergonomic public API

Step 2: Mount new Toaster in app
Step 3: Replace useToast() calls with toast.* API
Step 4: Remove old provider

Assert the observable output, database/cache side effect, emitted event or log, and one failure metric. Run the suite in CI with deterministic seeds and a production-shaped fixture so fs-061 cannot pass through a mocked happy path only.

title: Add cursor-based pagination to history feed with stale-while-revalidate refresh
business_context: Activity feed paginates with offset, causing duplicate/missed items when new activities arrive. Need cursor-based pagination with realtime prepend for new items.
constraints: Cursor must be stable under deletions
New items must appear at top without re-fetching old pages
Mid-page load must not lose scroll position
Backward compatible with old offset clients
reasoning: Compound cursor (createdAt, _id) ensures unique ordering and stable pagination. Endpoint accepts before=cursor. Realtime layer prepends new items via socket. Old offset clients still work via a compat branch.

after_state.description: Cursor pagination + realtime prepend + back-compat offset.

files.purpose: Cursor pagination via compound (createdAt, _id); back-compat offset branch
Cursor pagination + realtime prepend

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add cursor branch to controller
Step 2: Migrate frontend to cursors
Step 3: Add socket for realtime new items
Step 4: Sunset offset clients after monitoring
testing_strategy: Stable cursor: insert new items between page loads, assert no duplicates or skips. Realtime prepend: new item via socket appears at top. Compat: old offset clients still work. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-062 with failure artifacts attached Use a named fixture for fs-062 with tenant, actor, data volume, and failure-mode seeds. CI must assert output, persisted state, emitted event/log, and rollback behavior, then attach traces/screenshots or logs whenever this regression test fails.

title: Add audit-log retention per tenant tier with TTL indexes and archival
business_context: Audit logs grew to 200GB. Compliance requires 7y retention for enterprise but 90d is enough for self-serve. Need tier-aware retention with cost-effective archival.
constraints: Hot DB must keep last 30 days
Tier downgrade must not delete archived data
Recovery must be possible within 1 day

before_state.description: Single audit_logs collection with no retention policy.

reasoning: Hot collection has 30-day TTL via index. Nightly archival job picks 30-90 day entries, packs by day per tenant into parquet on S3, then deletes from hot DB. Tenant tier determines whether archival also has retention cap (enterprise=7y, self-serve=90d). Lookup endpoint reads hot first, then archival index if needed.

after_state.description: Hot 30-day TTL + nightly archival to S3 + tenant-tier retention policy.

files.purpose: TTL on at field ensures hot DB only keeps 30 days
Nightly archive of 1-day windows + tier-aware purge (prefer S3 lifecycle for actual deletion)
Query endpoint merges hot DB with archived S3 days

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add archival worker; runs nightly
Step 2: Add TTL index
Step 3: Migrate existing logs older than 30d to S3
Step 4: Configure S3 lifecycle policy per tier
Step 5: Audit query supports hybrid lookup
testing_strategy: Archival test: insert log dated 31d ago, run archival, assert appears in S3 and removed from DB. Query test: range spanning 60d returns archived + hot. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-064 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Add audit-log retention per tenant tier with TTL indexes and archival handles the demo path but misses the multi tenancy boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and render commit time are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

business_context: Users requested dark mode. App uses Tailwind with hardcoded light colors. Need full dark mode, system preference detection, manual override, persistence, smooth transition.

title: Reduce TTFB on cold lambda-style cold-start API by caching slow boot dependencies
business_context: Containers in serverless environment cold-start in 4s due to Mongoose+helmet+CRON setup. p99 TTFB hits 5s on scale-out. Need lazy-loading of non-critical deps and warm-up endpoint.
constraints: Critical deps must still init before first request

before_state.description: Eager initialization of all deps in app.ts.

issues: Jobs always start - even though only leader needs them

reasoning: Reorder boot. Start listening ASAP after critical deps (Mongo). Lazy-init non-critical (warmup, jobs) in background. Health endpoint returns 200 without depending on background work. Use Promise.all where independent. Warm-up runs but app already serving.

after_state.description: Critical-path init, background warmup, parallel where possible.

files.purpose: Start listening as soon as Mongo connected; background warm-up via dynamic import

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add readyz endpoint
Step 2: Defer non-critical init
Step 3: Configure k8s probes - liveness via healthz, readiness via readyz
Step 4: Monitor cold start TTFB
testing_strategy: Cold start: instrument boot time, assert listening within 1s. Background: assert warmup completes within 5s after listen. Health probes: assert healthz responds even during warmup. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. title: Add SSRF protection to URL fetcher used by webhook subscriptions and image proxy
business_context: Image proxy and webhook delivery fetch URLs from user input. Currently no validation. Attackers point at 169.254.169.254 (AWS metadata) to leak credentials. Need DNS+IP allowlisting and protocol restriction.
constraints: Must support customer webhook endpoints (any public IP)
Image proxy must reject private ranges and metadata services
reasoning: Validate URL: scheme must be http/https. Resolve DNS, check all resolved IPs are public (not RFC1918/loopback/link-local/AWS metadata). Re-validate on redirects - 30x with new URL must pass same checks. Set timeout. Limit redirect count.

after_state.description: DNS+IP validation, redirect re-validation, timeout, with controlled fetch.

files.purpose: DNS+IP validated fetch with redirect re-validation and timeout
Image proxy uses safeFetch and enforces content type + size cap

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add safeFetch alongside existing fetcher
Step 2: Migrate image proxy first
Step 3: Migrate webhook delivery
Step 4: Add ESLint rule banning direct fetch in url-fetching modules
Step 5: Penetration retest
testing_strategy: SSRF test: pass http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/, assert PRIVATE_IP_NOT_ALLOWED. Redirect test: site that 302s to private IP, assert blocked on second hop. Timeout test: slow endpoint > 5s, assert TIMEOUT. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-067 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Add SSRF protection to URL fetcher used by webhook subscriptions and image proxy handles the demo path but misses the security hardening boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and rollback elapsed minutes are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Decompose god-service into per-bounded-context services without changing API
business_context: orderService is 2400 lines containing pricing, inventory, fulfillment, notifications, and reporting. Onboarding takes weeks. Need split into bounded contexts without breaking 30 controllers calling it.
constraints: No API/controller changes in initial migration
Tests must pass at each step
Each new service must own its data

issues: One file owns pricing, inventory, persistence, notification
Cannot test pricing without notification side effects

reasoning: Identify bounded contexts: PricingService, InventoryService, OrderService (orchestrates), NotificationService. Each owns its domain data. OrderService coordinates. Migrate incrementally: split functions to new files but keep orderService re-exporting for back-compat.

after_state.description: Split services per context, OrderService orchestrates.

files.purpose: Pricing as its own context
Inventory atomic reserve via conditional decrement
Notifications routed through queue - decoupled from order flow
Order orchestrates pricing + inventory transactionally; fires notification after commit

migration_strategy: Step 1: Extract pricingService - delegate from orderService
Step 2: Extract inventoryService - delegate
Step 3: Extract notificationService
Step 4: Refactor place() to compose
Step 5: Each context can be moved to its own module/repo later
testing_strategy: Unit per service: pricing tests don't need notification mocks. Integration: place flow happy path + out-of-stock + payment failure rollback. Transactional test: kill mid-transaction, assert inventory restored. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records Use a named fixture for fs-068 with tenant, actor, data volume, and failure-mode seeds. CI must assert output, persisted state, emitted event/log, and rollback behavior, then attach traces/screenshots or logs whenever this regression test fails.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Decompose god-service into per-bounded-context services without changing API handles the demo path but misses the architecture migration boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and query p95 latency are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Add live form validation with backend uniqueness checks and debounced UX
business_context: Signup form checks email uniqueness on submit; users get an error after typing password and clicking submit. Need debounced live check with clear UI, but cannot hammer the server.
constraints: Must not let attacker enumerate emails via timing

issues: User wastes time on doomed form

reasoning: Debounced check (300ms) calls /check-email which returns boolean. Enumeration: server adds artificial delay to randomize timing + only returns boolean (not error message). Rate-limit per IP. Offline detection via navigator.onLine.

after_state.description: Debounced uniqueness check with timing protection, rate limit, offline handling.

files.purpose: Boolean availability with constant-ish timing
Debounced check with abort on input change
Form with live feedback; disables submit on taken

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add check-email endpoint with rate limit
Step 2: Ship hook + UI
Step 3: Monitor abuse - throttle aggressively if needed
Step 4: Consider adding CAPTCHA gating after N checks per IP
testing_strategy: Debounce test: type fast, assert only one network call after pause. Abort test: change email mid-flight, assert previous request aborted. Timing test: response time variance small enough to defeat timing oracle. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-069 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Add live form validation with backend uniqueness checks and debounced UX handles the demo path but misses the realtime e2e boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and query p95 latency are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Add product configurator with material/color swap and dynamic UV-mapped decals
business_context: E-commerce wants users to customize a 3D product: pick color, add a name decal, swap materials. Current product page is static image. Need interactive viewer with persistent state.
constraints: Each customization must persist in URL params for sharing
Must export final image for checkout preview
reasoning: r3f with GLB model. Color via uniform/material property. Decal via DecalGeometry from three/examples projected from camera. State synced to URL (color, decalText). Export via gl.toBlob.

after_state.description: Interactive configurator with color/decal customization and image export.

files.purpose: Configurator with URL-synced state, color/decal customization, image export

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add configurator behind feature flag
Step 2: Validate with marketing on top SKU
Step 3: Roll out to category
Step 4: Capture customizations during checkout
testing_strategy: URL share test: copy URL, paste in new window, assert same configuration loads. Export test: click export, assert PNG downloaded with correct rendering. Material swap test: change color, assert mesh updates without re-loading model. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-070 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Add product configurator with material/color swap and dynamic UV-mapped decals handles the demo path but misses the 3d integration boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and state drift incidents are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Add cross-org sharing of resources with explicit grants and read-only access
business_context: Customers in different orgs need to collaborate on a project. Currently strict tenant isolation prevents this. Need explicit cross-org grants: org A shares resource with org B read-only, with revocation and audit.
constraints: Audit trail of grants and accesses
reasoning: Add Grant model: { resourceType, resourceId, ownerOrgId, granteeOrgId, scope, status, createdBy }. Repository augments scoped query to also accept grants. Acceptance flow: owner creates grant in 'pending', grantee org admin approves. Audit on grant creation, approval, access via granted path.

after_state.description: Grant model + grant-aware repo with audit.

files.purpose: Grant model with status lifecycle
Repo returns owned OR granted resource with scope info
Grant lifecycle endpoints with audit
Owner UI to create grant

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add Grant model and endpoints
Step 2: Augment repo with grant-aware lookup
Step 3: Add UI for grant creation and approval
Step 4: Audit every shared access
Step 5: Add admin dashboard listing all grants
testing_strategy: Use a fixture for Add cross-org sharing of resources with explicit grants and read-only access that exercises the owned boundary, then add: happy-path test, unauthorized/invalid-input test, retry or concurrency simulation, persistence/contract assertion, and rollback/regression test. Assert the observable output, database/cache side effect, emitted event or log, and one failure metric. Run the suite in CI with deterministic seeds and a production-shaped fixture so fs-071 cannot pass through a mocked happy path only.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Add cross-org sharing of resources with explicit grants and read-only access handles the demo path but misses the multi tenancy boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and state drift incidents are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Replace blocking PDF rendering on request thread with worker pool + caching
business_context: Invoice PDF endpoint generates PDFs via Puppeteer in request thread - 800ms per request and blocks event loop on rendering. Need worker pool + caching with same-key dedup.
constraints: First request still gets fresh render if cache miss

issues: Launches a new Chromium per request - 500ms overhead

reasoning: Pool of long-lived puppeteer browsers (1-2). Each request gets a new page, render, dispose page. Cache by (invoiceId, version). Same-id concurrent requests share a promise. Worker pool via piscina if rendering pegs CPU.

after_state.description: Long-lived browser pool + LRU cache + in-flight dedup.

files.purpose: Long-lived browser pool with round-robin selection
Caching by (id, version) + in-flight dedup
Thin controller delegating to service

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add pool startup in server bootstrap
Step 2: Switch controller to service
Step 3: Measure p99 and Chromium memory
Step 4: Tune pool size
Step 5: Add graceful shutdown
testing_strategy: Cold render test: first call, assert reasonable latency. Cache hit: second call same id, assert near-zero latency. Concurrency dedup: 100 parallel requests same id, assert one renderPdf invocation. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-072 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Replace blocking PDF rendering on request thread with worker pool + caching handles the demo path but misses the performance crisis boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and query p95 latency are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Replace string-concat MongoDB query construction with parameterized builders to prevent NoSQL injection
business_context: Search endpoint passes req.query.filter through JSON.parse and spreads into Mongoose .find(). Attacker passes {$ne: null} or $where with code. Need safe query building with allowed-operators allowlist.
constraints: Must keep existing query expressiveness for legitimate users
Cannot break existing query shapes used by frontend

before_state.description: Direct spread of parsed JSON into .find().

reasoning: Define an allowed query schema per resource: which fields, which operators per field. Parse incoming JSON, validate against schema, reconstruct a safe Mongo filter. Reject operators not in allowlist. Tradeoff vs ditching JSON queries for a search DSL: more disruptive.

after_state.description: Schema-based query builder; allowlisted operators per field; rejects dangerous operators.

files.purpose: Parses and validates filter against a per-resource schema; rejects unsafe operators and fields
Uses safe builder with order-specific schema

migration_strategy: Step 1: Define schema per searched resource
Step 2: Replace .find(filter) calls one by one with buildSafeFilter
Step 3: Add lint rule disallowing JSON.parse → .find() patterns
Step 4: Security retest
testing_strategy: Injection test: {$where:'1'} returns 400. Field allowlist: query unknown field returns 400. Operator allowlist: try $regex (not in list) returns 400. Legitimate query still works. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. Assert both user-visible output and persistence/cache/event side effects. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-073 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Replace string-concat MongoDB query construction with parameterized builders to prevent NoSQL injection handles the demo path but misses the security hardening boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and authorization denial count are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Refactor scattered loading states into a unified async state machine pattern
business_context: Components have 5-6 useState flags for loading/error/data, often forgetting to reset between requests. Need standardized async hook with discriminated unions.
constraints: Must support refetch and stale state

issues: Stale state on id change between resolve

reasoning: useAsync hook with discriminated union state: idle | pending | success | error. Encapsulates AbortController. Supports refetch. Eliminates the 3-state-flag pattern.

after_state.description: useAsync with discriminated union and AbortController.

files.purpose: Generic async hook with abort, generation guard, and refetch
Component reads discriminated state - typecheck forces handling every case

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add hook
Step 2: Migrate components incrementally
Step 3: Remove manual loading/error state
Step 4: Add ESLint rule against three-flag pattern
testing_strategy: Generation guard test: change id mid-flight, assert previous result ignored. Abort test: unmount during pending, assert no setState warning. Refetch test: call refetch, assert new fetch fires. Use a production-shaped fixture with seeded tenant/user/data records. CI runs this as a deterministic regression suite for fs-074 with failure artifacts attached.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Refactor scattered loading states into a unified async state machine pattern handles the demo path but misses the state overhaul boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and policy block rate are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Add internationalization (i18n) with ICU MessageFormat, lazy locale loading, and server-side language detection
business_context: Product going to Japan, Germany, Brazil. App is English-only with hardcoded strings. Need i18n with ICU plurals, locale-specific date/number formats, lazy loading, RTL support for future Arabic.
constraints: Must support pluralization beyond simple n!=1
Server must detect locale for SSR/initial bundle
reasoning: Use intl-messageformat for ICU support. Resources keyed by locale, lazy loaded via dynamic import. Provider exposes t() function. Server detects Accept-Language and returns initial locale. Number/date via Intl.* APIs.

after_state.description: i18n provider, lazy locales, ICU MessageFormat, server detection.

files.purpose: English messages with ICU syntax
Japanese messages
Provider with lazy-loaded messages and ICU formatting
Component uses t() - locale agnostic
Server detects locale from Accept-Language; SSR uses res.locals.locale

migration_strategy: Step 1: Add provider + en messages; all keys still resolve
Step 2: Migrate strings to keys leaf-up
Step 3: Add ja/de/pt translations
Step 4: Server detects and renders initial locale
Step 5: Add locale switcher in UI
testing_strategy: Use a fixture for Add internationalization (i18n) with ICU MessageFormat, lazy locale loading, and server-side language detection that exercises the owned boundary, then add: happy-path test, unauthorized/invalid-input test, retry or concurrency simulation, persistence/contract assertion, and rollback/regression test. Assert the observable output, database/cache side effect, emitted event or log, and one failure metric. Run the suite in CI with deterministic seeds and a production-shaped fixture so fs-075 cannot pass through a mocked happy path only.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Add internationalization (i18n) with ICU MessageFormat, lazy locale loading, and server-side language detection handles the demo path but misses the feature addition with refactor boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and cache stale hit rate are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.
